entries.facilityRequired: Outpatient clinic or minor procedure room
entries.preparationInstructions: Clean the area with antiseptic. Informed consent required. Review medications (anticoagulants). Mark the lesion before procedure.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Keep wound clean and dry for 24-48 hours. Apply prescribed antibiotic ointment. Watch for signs of infection. Suture removal in 7-14 days depending on location.
contraindications: Patient on anticoagulants (relative - may need to hold)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for bleeding for 15-30 minutes post-procedure. Wound check at 48 hours if needed.

entries.preparationInstructions: Clean the area with antiseptic. Local anesthesia around the abscess. Informed consent required.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Pack the wound if indicated. Change packing as directed (usually daily). Warm compresses. Complete antibiotic course if prescribed. Follow up in 48-72 hours for wound check.
contraindications: Peritonsillar or deep neck abscess (requires ENT/surgical consult)
preProcedureRequirements: Assessment of abscess size and depth
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for bleeding and hemodynamic stability for 15-30 minutes. Wound culture if indicated.

entries.preparationInstructions: Clean injection site with antiseptic. Review for anticoagulant use. Ultrasound guidance if available. Confirm no active joint infection.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Rest the joint for 24-48 hours. Avoid strenuous activity for 48 hours. Apply ice as needed. Monitor for signs of infection (increasing pain, swelling, fever).
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 15 minutes post-injection for allergic reaction or vasovagal response.

entries.facilityRequired: Endoscopy suite or ambulatory surgery center
entries.preparationInstructions: Bowel preparation with prescribed solution (e.g., PEG solution) day before. Clear liquid diet 24 hours prior. NPO after midnight. Arrange transportation home. Hold anticoagulants as directed.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Rest for remainder of the day. Resume diet gradually starting with clear liquids. Do not drive for 24 hours after sedation. Watch for bleeding, severe abdominal pain, or fever. Contact physician immediately if symptoms occur.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with sedation consent
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Recovery room monitoring for 30-60 minutes. Monitor vitals, oxygen saturation, level of consciousness. Discharge when Aldrete score is adequate.

entries.facilityRequired: Endoscopy suite or ambulatory surgery center
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours before procedure. Arrange transportation home. Hold anticoagulants as directed. Remove dentures before procedure.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Do not eat or drink until gag reflex returns (usually 1-2 hours). Start with clear liquids then advance diet. Do not drive for 24 hours after sedation. Watch for difficulty swallowing, chest pain, or bleeding.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with sedation consent
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Recovery room monitoring for 30-60 minutes. Monitor vitals, oxygen saturation, gag reflex return.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO 6-8 hours prior. Pre-procedure labs (CBC, CMP, coagulation, type and screen). IV access. Shave groin/wrist as needed. Allergy assessment for contrast. Hold metformin. Hydration protocol for renal protection.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Bed rest with affected limb straight for 2-6 hours (femoral) or 1-2 hours (radial). Monitor access site for bleeding or hematoma. Resume fluids to flush contrast. Resume medications as directed.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with detailed risk discussion
preProcedureRequirements: Pre-procedure labs (CBC, CMP, PT/INR, type and screen)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Continuous cardiac monitoring for 4-6 hours. Vital signs every 15 minutes for first hour. Access site checks every 15 minutes. Monitor urine output. Serial ECGs if indicated.

entries.facilityRequired: Ambulatory surgery center or operating room
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours prior. Pre-procedure labs (CBC, pregnancy test, blood type). IV access. Empty bladder before procedure.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Expect vaginal bleeding and cramping for several days. Use pads (no tampons) for 2 weeks. No sexual intercourse for 2 weeks. No heavy lifting for 1 week. Follow up in 2 weeks. Seek care for heavy bleeding, fever, or foul-smelling discharge.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with anesthesia consent
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Recovery room monitoring for 1-2 hours. Monitor vitals, vaginal bleeding, and pain level. Discharge when stable and able to tolerate oral intake.

entries.facilityRequired: Operating room (hospital or ambulatory surgery center)
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours prior. Pre-procedure labs (CBC, CMP, coagulation). Hold anticoagulants as directed. Arrange transportation and post-operative assistance. Pre-operative MRI if not already done.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: RICE protocol (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation). Weight bearing as tolerated with crutches. Pain management as prescribed. Begin physical therapy as directed. Keep wounds clean and dry. Follow up in 1-2 weeks for wound check.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with anesthesia consent
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Post-anesthesia care unit (PACU) monitoring for 1-2 hours. Monitor vitals, neurovascular status of the limb, drain output if placed, pain level.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours prior. Pre-procedure labs (CBC, coagulation, ABG if indicated). Review imaging. Discontinue anticoagulants as directed. Pre-medication with atropine and sedation as ordered.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: NPO until gag and cough reflex return (1-2 hours). Monitor for respiratory distress. No driving for 24 hours. Report hemoptysis, fever, or increasing shortness of breath.
contraindications: Severe hypoxia refractory to supplemental oxygen
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with sedation consent
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Continuous pulse oximetry for 2 hours. Monitor for respiratory distress, hemoptysis, fever. Chest X-ray if pneumothorax suspected.

entries.anesthesiaType: Local anesthesia (topical lidocaine gel) or conscious sedation
entries.facilityRequired: Procedure room or ambulatory surgery center
entries.preparationInstructions: Urine culture to rule out active UTI. May need to hold anticoagulants. Hydrate well before procedure. Empty bladder before if not catheterized.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Increase fluid intake for 24-48 hours. Mild burning with urination is normal for 24-48 hours. Light pink-tinged urine may occur. Report heavy bleeding, inability to urinate, fever, or chills.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for urinary retention and gross hematuria for 30-60 minutes post-procedure.

entries.facilityRequired: Operating room or ambulatory surgery center with ophthalmic equipment
entries.preparationInstructions: Pre-operative eye measurements (biometry). Dilating drops on the day. NPO if sedation planned. Discontinue anticoagulants as directed by surgeon. Antibiotic drops 1-3 days before surgery if prescribed.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Wear eye shield at night for 1 week. Use prescribed eye drops as directed (antibiotic and anti-inflammatory). Avoid rubbing the eye. No heavy lifting or bending for 1-2 weeks. No swimming for 2 weeks. Follow up next day and at 1 week.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with IOL selection discussion
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor intraocular pressure 1-2 hours post-procedure. Check for wound integrity and anterior chamber depth. Same-day discharge typical.

entries.facilityRequired: Mohs surgery suite with on-site histopathology laboratory
entries.preparationInstructions: Review medications (anticoagulants). Clean face/site of makeup. Plan for extended visit (multiple stages may be needed). Eat breakfast before arrival. Informed consent with discussion of reconstruction options.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Pressure dressing for 24-48 hours. Keep wound clean and moist with prescribed ointment. Change dressing daily. Avoid strenuous activity for 48 hours. Suture removal in 5-14 days depending on location.
risksAndComplications: Need for flap or graft reconstruction
contraindications: Tumors with perineural invasion extending beyond surgical field
Patient unable to tolerate prolonged procedure
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with reconstruction options discussed
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for bleeding after each stage. Final wound check before discharge. Follow-up wound check in 1 week.

entries.facilityRequired: Fluoroscopy suite or operating room with C-arm
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6 hours if sedation planned. Hold anticoagulants as directed. Review MRI before procedure. No active infection. Blood glucose management in diabetics.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Rest for 24 hours. Avoid driving day of procedure. May have temporary leg weakness or numbness. Apply ice to injection site. Monitor blood glucose if diabetic (steroids may raise glucose). Avoid bathing/soaking for 24 hours.
risksAndComplications: Allergic reaction to steroid or contrast
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for 30-60 minutes. Check lower extremity sensation and motor function. Vital signs. Discharge when stable and able to ambulate.

entries.facilityRequired: Operating room (hospital or ambulatory surgery center)
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours prior. Pre-operative labs (CBC, coagulation). Anesthesia evaluation. Discontinue NSAIDs and aspirin 2 weeks prior. Arrange post-operative care and transportation.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Soft/liquid diet for 10-14 days. Adequate fluid intake essential. Pain management (avoid aspirin/NSAIDs). Rest for 10-14 days. No strenuous activity for 2 weeks. Seek emergency care for active bleeding from mouth or throat.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent with anesthesia consent
preProcedureRequirements: Post-operative care instructions reviewed with patient/family
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: PACU monitoring for 2-4 hours. Monitor for bleeding, airway patency, nausea/vomiting, pain control, and ability to tolerate fluids. May require overnight observation in children.

entries.facilityRequired: Dental clinic or oral surgery office
entries.preparationInstructions: Review medications (anticoagulants, bisphosphonates). Dental radiograph of affected tooth. Informed consent. Assess for infection requiring pre-treatment antibiotics.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Bite on gauze for 30-45 minutes. No rinsing, spitting, or using straws for 24 hours. Soft foods for 24-48 hours. Avoid smoking for at least 48 hours. Gentle salt water rinses after 24 hours. Take prescribed pain medication as directed.
risksAndComplications: Nerve damage (numbness of lip, tongue, or chin)
contraindications: Active pericoronitis with trismus (may need antibiotics first)
Radiation therapy to jaw (risk of osteoradionecrosis)
Bisphosphonate therapy (risk of osteonecrosis - requires specialist referral)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor for 15-30 minutes for hemostasis. Verify clot formation. Review post-operative instructions with patient.
